mcqs.question: What is the output of the command: date +%Y%m%d
mcqs.explanation: The format %Y%m%d produces YYYYMMDD with no separators. For January 15, 2024, the output would be 20240115.

mcqs.question: Which command displays the current time in 12-hour format with AM/PM?
mcqs.explanation: %r displays time in 12-hour format with AM/PM (e.g., 02:30:45 PM). %T is 24-hour HH:MM:SS, %R is HH:MM, %s is epoch seconds.

mcqs.question: What is required to set the system date using the date command?

options.text: Any user can set the date
Only the root user or sudo privileges

mcqs.explanation: Setting the system date and time is a privileged operation. Only the superuser (root) or users with appropriate sudo privileges can modify the system clock.

mcqs.question: What does the command date -u return?
mcqs.explanation: The -u option displays the time in UTC (Coordinated Universal Time), regardless of the system's timezone setting.

mcqs.question: Which format specifier displays the abbreviated month name?
mcqs.explanation: %b displays the abbreviated month name (e.g., Jan). %B displays the full month name. %m displays the month as a number (01-12). %M displays minutes (00-59).

mcqs.question: What will date +%s return?

options.text: Number of seconds since January 1, 1970 UTC

mcqs.explanation: %s displays the Unix epoch time - the number of seconds that have elapsed since January 1, 1970, 00:00:00 UTC.

mcqs.question: How do you display the date for '2 days ago'?
mcqs.explanation: The -d or --date option allows specifying a date string. "2 days ago" is parsed to display the date from 2 days before the current date.

mcqs.question: Which command converts epoch timestamp 1704067200 to human-readable format?
mcqs.explanation: The @ symbol before a number indicates it is an epoch timestamp. date -d @1704067200 converts it to the local timezone representation.

mcqs.question: What is the output of: date +"Today is %A, %B %d, %Y"?
mcqs.explanation: %A gives full weekday name, %B gives full month name, %d gives day with leading zero, %Y gives 4-digit year. Output: "Today is Monday, January 15, 2024"

mcqs.question: Which option displays the date last modified of a file in Unix?
mcqs.explanation: The date command itself doesn't display file timestamps. Use 'ls -l filename' to see the modification time, or 'stat filename' for detailed timestamp information.

mcqs.question: What does %j display in the date command?
mcqs.explanation: %j displays the day of the year as a three-digit number padded with leading zeros (001-366). Sunday is 0 in %w and 7 in %u.

mcqs.question: Which command displays only the time in HH:MM format?
mcqs.explanation: %R displays time in HH:MM format (24-hour). %T displays HH:MM:SS. %t is a tab character. %X is locale's time representation.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of TZ environment variable with date?

options.text: It has no effect on date command
It sets the timezone for date output

mcqs.explanation: The TZ (Timezone) environment variable affects how date displays time. For example, TZ=EST date displays Eastern Standard Time.

mcqs.question: Which format specifier displays the week number of the year (ISO)?
mcqs.explanation: %V displays the ISO week number (01-53). %U is Sunday-based week number, %W is Monday-based week number. %G is ISO year for %V.

mcqs.question: What happens when you run: date -s "2024-12-25 10:30:00" as a regular user?

options.text: It displays the new date without changing system time
It creates a cron job for that date

mcqs.explanation: Regular users (non-root) cannot modify system time. Running date -s without sudo privileges results in a "permission denied" error.
